_: Annual meteor showers (established, recurring). Each: {id, name, name_he, peak_month, peak_day, active_from, active_to, radiant_ra(hours), radiant_dec(deg), radiant_con, zhr, velocity_kms, parent_body, moonless_best(bool, informational), note_he}. peak_day is the typical maximum date; it shifts ±1 day year to year. ZHR (Zenithal Hourly Rate) is the IDEAL count under a moonless Bortle-1 sky with the radiant overhead — real counts at the horizon / with Moon are lower.
source: International Meteor Organization (IMO) Meteor Shower Calendar + IAU Meteor Data Center established-shower list. Peak dates, ZHR, radiant coordinates and parent bodies are the standard published values.
license: Reference values from published IMO/IAU sources (factual catalog data, freely citable).
site_note: At Larkmont (lat 34.0°N, Bortle 3) the Quadrantids, Lyrids, Perseids, Geminids and Eta Aquariids are the strongest. Southern-radiant showers culminate low; the listed ZHR is the all-sky ideal, not what is seen here.
honesty: ZHR and peak dates are MEASURED/established averages. Actual visible rate at this site on a given night is lower and depends on Moon phase, radiant altitude and exact peak timing — treat as a planning estimate.

showers.name: Quadrantids
showers.note_he: A sharp, short peak (just a few hours); a northern radiant (Boötes) — low until after midnight, rising high toward dawn from Larkmont.

showers.name: Lyrids
showers.note_he: An old spring shower; occasional isolated bright outbursts.

showers.name: Eta Aquariids
showers.note_he: Debris from Halley's Comet; best seen before sunrise, with a low eastern radiant.

showers.name: Southern Delta Aquariids
showers.note_he: A southern radiant that rises relatively high from Larkmont's latitude — well observed from here.

showers.name: Perseids
showers.note_he: The famous summer shower; many bright meteors with trains. Peaks on the nights of August 11–13.

showers.name: Orionids
showers.note_he: Halley's Comet's second shower; fast meteors.

showers.name: Southern Taurids
showers.note_he: A low rate but known for slow, bright fireballs.

showers.name: Northern Taurids
showers.note_he: A sister branch to the Southern Taurids; also a source of fireballs.

showers.name: Leonids
showers.note_he: Very fast meteors; about once every ~33 years capable of a meteor storm (the last in 1999–2002).

showers.name: Geminids
showers.note_he: The densest shower of the year; bright, colorful meteors. The peak is on the night of December 13→14 — the radiant rises already in the evening and is high after midnight, excellent from Larkmont.

showers.name: Ursids
showers.note_he: A quiet winter shower around the winter solstice; a very northern radiant.

showers.name: Alpha Capricornids
showers.note_he: A low rate but slow, bright fireballs; overlaps in time with the Delta Aquariids.

showers.name: Draconids
showers.note_he: Very slow meteors; usually weak, but capable of sharp outbursts in select years. Best seen right after sunset.
